original_abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) characterized by high water uptake capacities and robust hydrothermal stability are increasingly being recognized as premier candidates for low-temperature heat transformation. These materials hold significant promise for advancing the efficiency of sustainable cooling and heating technologies, including thermally driven adsorption chillers and adsorption heat pumps.
humanized_abstract: Recently, a new class of materials entitled as “MOFs with high H2O uptake capacity and hydrothermal stability at low temperatures” known as Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with high water uptake capacities and robust hydrothermal stability have been intensively investigated at low temperatures due to their superior performances. MOFs with high water uptake capacity and excellent hydrothermal stability at low temperatures have been found to have great potential for improving the performance and efficiency of the sustainable cooling and heating systems such as thermally driven adsorption chillers and adsorption heat pumps.
